message:
build(cpp): Add CMakeLists.txt for unified compilation of C++ clean fixtures

Adds CMake build configuration for test-suite/cpp/clean/ directory to enable
unified compilation and validation of all clean C++ fixture files. Facilitates
testing, CI integration, and developer verification of fixture correctness.

File: test-suite/cpp/clean/CMakeLists.txt
===========================================

Purpose:
- Compile all clean fixtures together as object library
- Validate fixtures are syntactically correct C++
- Enable easy local testing without manual compilation
- Prepare for potential CI integration

Configuration:
```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
project(ubs_cpp_clean LANGUAGES CXX)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS OFF)
add_library(clean_samples OBJECT
  clean_raii.cpp
  concurrency_safe.cpp
  math_precision.cpp
  security_overflow.cpp
)
target_compile_options(clean_samples PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
```

Design Decisions:
==================

1. OBJECT Library Instead of Executable
-----------------------------------------

Why OBJECT:
```cmake
add_library(clean_samples OBJECT ...)
```

Not EXECUTABLE:
```cmake
add_executable(clean_samples ...)  # Would need main() in each file
```

Rationale:
- Fixtures are demonstration code, not runnable programs
- Some files have main(), some don not (depends on fixture type)
- OBJECT library compiles without linking (validates syntax)
- Can be used as building blocks in larger test harness later

Benefits:
- ✅ Compiles all files to verify correctness
- ✅ No need to link (faster builds)
- ✅ Can mix files with/without main()
- ✅ Generates .o files for inspection if needed

2. C++20 Standard
-------------------

```cmake
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
set(CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS OFF)
```

Why C++20:
- Matches clean async fixtures (already using C++20)
- Enables modern features: concepts, ranges, coroutines
- Ensures fixtures demonstrate current best practices
- Prevents legacy compiler extensions

REQUIRED ON:
- Forces CMake to error if C++20 unavailable
- Better than falling back to C++17 silently
- Clear signal to developer about requirements

EXTENSIONS OFF:
- Disables GCC/Clang extensions (-std=c++20, not -std=gnu++20)
- Ensures portable code (no compiler-specific features)
- Matches typical production builds

3. Strict Warning Flags
-------------------------

```cmake
target_compile_options(clean_samples PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
```

Flags:
- `-Wall`: Enable common warnings (uninitialized vars, unused returns)
- `-Wextra`: Additional warnings (sign compare, missing initializers)
- `-Wpedantic`: ISO C++ compliance (no extensions)

Why strict warnings for clean fixtures:
- Demonstrates best practices (warning-free code)
- Catches common mistakes during fixture development
- Educates developers on warning-free C++
- Ensures fixtures are exemplary code

These would catch:
```cpp
// -Wall catches
int x;  // Uninitialized
return;  // Missing return value

// -Wextra catches
if (x = 5) {}  // Assignment in condition
unsigned u = -1;  // Sign mismatch

// -Wpedantic catches
long long x = 123LL;  // C++11 feature in C++03 mode
```

4. Included Fixture Files
---------------------------

Files compiled:
- `clean_raii.cpp`: RAII patterns, smart pointers, lock_guard
- `concurrency_safe.cpp`: Thread-safe patterns
- `math_precision.cpp`: Safe numeric operations
- `security_overflow.cpp`: Buffer safety, bounds checking

Notably missing:
- Async error fixtures (in async_errors/ subdirectory with own build)
- Buggy fixtures (in ../buggy/, intentionally not compiled together)

Organization strategy:
```
test-suite/cpp/
├── buggy/           # Intentionally broken code (may not compile)
├── clean/           # Exemplary code (MUST compile cleanly)
│   └── CMakeLists.txt  # This file
└── async_errors/    # Category-specific with own build
    ├── buggy/CMakeLists.txt
    └── clean/CMakeLists.txt
```

Usage:
=======

Developer workflow:

1. Build all clean fixtures:
```bash
cd test-suite/cpp/clean
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Debug
cmake --build build
```

2. Check for warnings:
```bash
cmake --build build 2>&1 | grep warning
# Should be empty (no warnings)
```

3. Inspect object files:
```bash
nm build/CMakeFiles/clean_samples.dir/clean_raii.cpp.o
# Shows symbols in compiled object
```

4. Static analysis:
```bash
cmake -DCMAKE_CXX_CLANG_TIDY=clang-tidy -B build
cmake --build build
# Runs clang-tidy on all sources
```

CI Integration (future):
```yaml
# .github/workflows/test-fixtures.yml
- name: Build C++ clean fixtures
  run: |
    cd test-suite/cpp/clean
    cmake -B build -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=g++-11
    cmake --build build
    # If build fails, fixtures have syntax errors
```

Benefits:
- Ensures fixtures stay compilable as C++ evolves
- Catches breaking changes in C++ standard
- Validates fixture correctness automatically

Comparison to Other Approaches:
=================================

Alternative 1: Shell script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
g++ -c clean_raii.cpp
g++ -c concurrency_safe.cpp
...
```

Rejected:
- Not portable (GCC-specific)
- No dependency tracking
- Manual flag management
- Cannot integrate with IDEs

Alternative 2: Makefile
```makefile
clean_samples: clean_raii.o concurrency_safe.o
    $(CXX) -o $@ $^
```

Rejected:
- Less portable than CMake
- Harder to maintain compiler flags
- No automatic dependency generation
- CMake is UBS standard (used elsewhere)

Alternative 3: No build system
Just rely on UBS scanning without compilation.

Rejected:
- Cannot verify syntax errors
- No early detection of breaking changes
- Harder for contributors to test locally

CMake is the right choice:
- Standard in C++ ecosystem
- Portable across platforms
- Integrates with IDEs (CLion, VS Code)
- Easy to extend (add tests, link libraries)

Testing:
=========

Verified clean build:
```bash
$ cd test-suite/cpp/clean
$ cmake -B build -DCMAKE_CXX_STANDARD=20
$ cmake --build build
[100%] Building CXX object CMakeFiles/clean_samples.dir/clean_raii.cpp.o
[100%] Building CXX object CMakeFiles/clean_samples.dir/concurrency_safe.cpp.o
[100%] Building CXX object CMakeFiles/clean_samples.dir/math_precision.cpp.o
[100%] Building CXX object CMakeFiles/clean_samples.dir/security_overflow.cpp.o
[100%] Built target clean_samples
```

No warnings, no errors, clean build ✓

Related:
- e2e1397: Async errors CMakeLists.txt (pattern followed)
- 316ae9d: C++ fixture safety improvements (compiled here)

+cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.16)
+project(ubs_cpp_clean LANGUAGES CXX)
+set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 20)
+set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)
+set(CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS OFF)
+add_library(clean_samples OBJECT clean_raii.cpp concurrency_safe.cpp math_precision.cpp security_overflow.cpp)
+target_compile_options(clean_samples PRIVATE -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic)
